title: Estate Manager
description:
Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition

Welcome to OneSG Holding Pte Ltd, a leading provider of property management services. Our team of experienced professionals is committed to delivering exceptional service tailored to the unique needs of strata living environments and condominiums. We offer a wide range of customized management services to ensure that our clients receive the highest levels of support in every situation. Contact us today to discover how our tailored property management solutions can meet your specific needs. OneSG Holding Pte Ltd is a trusted Managing Agent Services company dedicated to providing top-notch property management services. We focus on Mechanical, Electrical & Fire Management, Building Defects & Facade Management, R&R Works & Project Management, and Energy Efficiency Management, establishing ourselves as a reliable partner for property owners. Our highly committed HQ provides business support and technical expertise, while our established procedures and SOPs adhere to market best practices. Our team of experienced and qualified professionals are specialists in their respective fields and knowledgeable in legislations and regulations relating to MCST management and building-related compliances. We value the trust and confidence our clients place in us and endeavor to uphold that trust should we be appointed as Managing Agent for MCST. OneSG offers a full spectrum of property management services with in-depth knowledge of project management, including water tank maintenance replacement, lift modernization, and other essential asset enhancement initiatives for aging MCST estates. With years of property management experience, the dedicated staff at OneSG have become true experts in the local Managing Agent Services market with Management Corporation Strata Title, MCSTs. We believe in Outcome/Performance-Based facility management systems. As a property management company, we are dedicated to doing whatever it takes to help you achieve your estate goals with professional and digital tools in strata living environments or condominiums. Learn more about OneSG.

Job Summary

The Building Manager leads the comprehensive management and operational oversight of the building, ensuring compliance with statutory requirements and delivering high standards of maintenance and service. Reporting to senior leadership, you will drive financial stewardship, coordinate cross-functional teams, and engage stakeholders to enhance property value and resident satisfaction.

Responsibilities

  • Serve as the primary liaison to the council and residents by actively listening to inquiries and delivering effective solutions to improve community satisfaction
  • Maintain a professional and courteous demeanor in all communications to foster a positive and supportive environment
  • Manage overall office administration to ensure organized and efficient operations
  • Advise the Corporation on all property-related matters, ensuring compliance with bylaws, laws, and statutory requirements
  • Convene, attend, and document minutes for Council, Sub-committee, general, and extraordinary meetings to support transparent governance
  • Handle inquiries, complaints, and correspondence related to the Corporation, property, and subsidiary proprietors with timely and accurate responses
  • Coordinate and engage contractors, solicitors, auditors, architects, engineers, and other professionals with prior Corporation approval to execute maintenance and project works
  • Act on behalf of the Corporation in responding to notices or orders from public or statutory authorities concerning the property
  • Collect and recover all monies due to the Corporation, initiating legal proceedings when necessary to secure payments
  • Ensure timely payments to contractors and service providers for property upkeep, statutory charges, licenses, professional fees, rent, insurance, and taxes
  • Maintain accurate financial records of all monies received and expended on behalf of the Corporation
  • Prepare and submit annual budgets to determine expenses and recommend contribution rates for management and sinking funds
  • Deliver financial reports on contractual matters governing the property to the Corporation for informed decision-making
  • Review service contracts for cost-effectiveness and advise the Council on necessary changes or renewals
  • Monitor utilities consumption and implement timely actions to achieve cost savings or recover overpayments
  • Oversee day-to-day building operations, ensuring all maintenance and administrative staff perform to the highest standards expected by clients and subsidiary proprietors
  • Lead teams responsible for maintenance, finance, security, fire safety, and car park management to deliver seamless property services
  • Ensure all contract works and routine servicing comply with contractual agreements and quality standards
  • Review contracts, agreements, and insurance policies before expiry and recommend renewals or initiate quotations and tenders
  • Coordinate council and general meetings to facilitate effective stakeholder engagement
  • Manage the property’s condition to comply with all relevant legislation, standards, and requirements, including BMSMA and cautionary practices
  • Track and resolve estate matters arising from AGM/EOGM, Council, Sub-committee meetings, and resident feedback from planning through execution to closure
  • Develop estate work plans with Portfolio Manager guidance for approved projects, major events, and tasks aligned with AGM cycles
  • Assist in preparing specifications, calling tenders, evaluating bids, and recommending contractors to the Corporation
  • Conduct regular property inspections to verify maintenance quality and service provider performance
  • Implement preventive maintenance programs approved by the Corporation to sustain property condition
  • Manage site staff performance, behavior, and attitudes to uphold the Corporation’s best interests
  • Maintain regular communication with Portfolio Manager and Business Unit Head on major issues, breakdowns, complaints, project progress, emergencies, and liabilities
  • Perform additional duties as assigned by supervisors to support operational excellence
